member.name: M:EleWise.ELMA.Web.Mvc.Components.NotificationFunctions.GetEntityUrl(EleWise.TemplateGenerator.Functions.FunctionEvaluationContext)
member.summary: Получить ссылку на детальную страницу сущности.
member.remarks:
* - Обязательные параметры Параметры: [0]*: Объект (IEntity)
Результат: Строка, содержащая сформированную ссылку на детальную страницу сущности
Пример: Пусть Task - переменная типа "Базовый класс задачи", ИД которой равен 603. Чтобы сформировать ссылку на детальную страницу сущности: {GetEntityUrl({$Task})} Результат: "/Tasks/Task/Execute/603"

member.name: T:EleWise.ELMA.Web.Content.IContentAction
member.summary:
Действие
Что собой представлят
Какие атрибуты: - отображение -- текст (испольуется в элементах меню, кнопках тулбара, ссылках на страницах) -- икона (используется в элементах меню, кнопках тулбара; следует предусмотреть размеры для меню и тулбара) -- идеетификатор (используется при обработке разметки вредакторе и на сервере) - действие -- ссылка на станицу или javascript код (или что то еще типа отправки формы???) -- дополнительный HTML код (всплывающий popup, js или еще что-то ЭТО ДОЛЖНО РАБОТАТЬ ТАК ЖЕ В Ajax Режиме! -> RenderZone для renderzone)

member.name: T:Orchard.Environment.Extensions.Loaders.DynamicExtensionLoader.ReloadWorkaround
member.summary: We should be able to support reloading multiple version of a compiled module from a ".csproj" file in the same AppDomain. However, we are currently running into a limitation with NHibernate getting confused when a type name is present in multiple assemblies loaded in an AppDomain. So, until a solution is found, any change to a ".csproj" file of an active module requires an AppDomain restart. The purpose of this class is to keep track of all .csproj files monitored until an AppDomain restart.

member.name: M:Orchard.Environment.Extensions.Loaders.IExtensionLoader.GetVirtualPathDependencies(Orchard.FileSystems.Dependencies.DependencyDescriptor)
member.summary: Return the list of dependencies (as virtual path) of the given module. If any of the dependency returned in the list is updated, a component depending on the assembly produced for the module must be re-compiled. For example, Razor or WebForms views needs to be recompiled when a dependency of a module changes.

member.name: M:Orchard.FileSystems.VirtualPath.DefaultVirtualPathProvider.IsMalformedVirtualPath(System.String)
member.summary:
We want to reject path that contains ".." going outside of the application root. ToAppRelative does that already, but we want to do the same while avoiding exceptions.
Note: This method doesn't detect all cases of malformed paths, it merely checks for *some* cases of malformed paths, so this is not a replacement for full virtual path verification through VirtualPathUtilty methods. In other words, !IsMalformed does *not* imply "IsWellformed".

dynamic shape = New.ShapeName(Parameter: myVar)

member.name: T:Orchard.Mvc.ViewEngines.Razor.DefaultRazorCompilationEvents
member.summary: The purpose of this class is to notify the Razor View Engine of Module and Theme dependencies when compiling Views, so that the Razor Views build provider will add proper assembly references when calling the compiler. For example, if Module A depends on Module Bar and some other 3rd party DLL "Foo.dll", we will notify the Razor View Engine of the following dependencies: * BuildManager.GetCompiledAssembly("~/Modules/Bar/Bar.csproj"); * Assembly.Load("Foo");

member.name: P:Orchard.Owin.OwinMiddlewareRegistration.Priority
member.summary: Gets or sets the priority value that decides the order in which such objects are processed. I.e. "0" will run before "10", but registrations without a priority value will run before the ones that have it set. Note that this priority notation is the same as the one for shape placement (so you can e.g. use ":before").

member.name: T:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLock
member.summary: Represents a distributed lock returned by IDistributedLockService. The owner of the lock should call Dispose() on an instance of this interface to release the distributed lock.

member.name: T:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService
member.summary: Provides functionality to acquire and release tenant-wide locks which are distributed across all instances in a web farm.
member.remarks: Distributed locks can be used to protect critical sections that should only ever be executed by a single thread of execution across a whole web farm. The distributed locks returned by this service are reentrant, i.e. the owner of a lock can reacquire it multiple times, and must also release it (dispose of it) as many times as it was acquired for the lock to be released.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService.TryAcquireLock(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLock@)
member.summary: Tries to acquire a named distributed lock within the current tenant.
member.param: The name of the lock to acquire.

The maximum amount of time the lock should be held before automatically expiring. This is a safeguard in case the owner fails to release the lock. If null is specified, the lock never automatically expires.

The amount of time to wait for the lock to be acquired. Passing TimeSpan.Zero will cause the method to return immediately. Passing null will cause the method to block indefinitely until a lock can be acquired.
This out parameter will be assigned the acquired lock if successful.
member.returns: true if the lock was successfully acquired, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService.AcquireLock(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Acquires a named distributed lock within the current tenant or throws if the lock cannot be acquired.
member.param: The name of the lock to acquire.

The maximum amount of time the lock should be held before automatically expiring. This is a safeguard in case the owner fails to release the lock. If null is specified, the lock never automatically expires.

The amount of time to wait for the lock to be acquired. Passing TimeSpan.Zero will cause the method to return immediately. Passing null will cause the method to block indefinitely until a lock can be acquired.
member.exception: This method throws a TimeoutException if the lock could not be acquired within the specified timeout period.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.DistributedLockServiceExtensions.TryAcquireLock(Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService,System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLock@)
member.summary: Tries to immediately acquire a named distributed lock with a given expiration time within the current tenant.
member.param: The name of the lock to acquire.

The maximum amount of time the lock should be held before automatically expiring. This is a safeguard in case the owner fails to release the lock. If null is specified, the lock never automatically expires.
This out parameter will be assigned the acquired lock if successful.
member.returns: true if the lock could be immediately acquired, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.DistributedLockServiceExtensions.TryAcquireLock(Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService,System.String,Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLock@)
member.summary: Tries to immediately acquire a named distributed lock with no expiration time within the current tenant.
member.param: The name of the lock to acquire.
This out parameter will be assigned the acquired lock if successful.
member.returns: true if the lock could be immediately acquired, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.DistributedLockServiceExtensions.AcquireLock(Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService,System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Acquires a named distributed lock with a given expiration time within the current tenant.
member.param: The name of the lock to acquire.

The maximum amount of time the lock should be held before automatically expiring. This is a safeguard in case the owner fails to release the lock. If null is specified, the lock never automatically expires.
member.remarks: This method blocks indefinitely until the lock can be acquired.

member.name: M:Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.DistributedLockServiceExtensions.AcquireLock(Orchard.Tasks.Locking.Services.IDistributedLockService,System.String)
member.summary: Acquires a named distributed lock with no expiration time within the current tenant.
member.param: The name to use for the lock.
member.remarks: This method blocks indefinitely until the lock can be acquired.

member.name: T:Orchard.UI.Zones.ZoneOnDemand
member.remarks: InterfaceProxyBehavior() NilBehavior() => return Nil on GetMember and GetIndex in all cases ZoneOnDemandBehavior(_zoneFactory, _parent, name) => when a zone (Shape) is created, replace itself with the zone so that Layout.ZoneName is no more equal to Nil

member.summary: Sort a collection of elements "by dependency order". By passing a lambda which determines if an element is a dependency of another, this algorithm will return the collection of elements sorted so that a given element in the sequence doesn't depend on any other element further in the sequence.

member.summary: We need a custom string hash code function, because .NET string.GetHashCode() function is not guaranteed to be constant across multiple executions.
